budget_wines.name: Maison Louis Latour Bourgogne Chardonnay
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Maison Louis Latour, Beaune; widely distributed across France, the UK and the United States.
budget_wines.description: A regional Bourgogne Blanc that is the easiest entry to white Burgundy. Unoaked, crisp green-apple and citrus Chardonnay from across the region, a reliable house benchmark under twenty euros.
budget_wines.tip: The plain Bourgogne Chardonnay is the value play; trade up to the Macon-Lugny only when you want a touch more flesh from the southern fruit.

budget_wines.name: Maison Louis Latour Macon-Lugny
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Maison Louis Latour, Beaune; one of the most widely available Maconnais whites internationally.
budget_wines.description: Chardonnay from the warm limestone slopes around Lugny in the southern Maconnais. Rounder and riper than basic Bourgogne Blanc, with orchard fruit and a soft finish, dependable value for the table.
budget_wines.tip: A safe house white in any French wine shop; serve lightly chilled with roast chicken or a goat-cheese salad.

budget_wines.name: Cave de Lugny Macon-Villages
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Cave de Lugny cooperative cellar, Lugny; exported widely through supermarket and merchant channels.
budget_wines.description: The cooperative that makes roughly a third of all Macon-Villages turns out a clean, fruit-forward Chardonnay at supermarket prices. The everyday white Burgundy benchmark for under fifteen euros.
budget_wines.tip: Look for the single-vineyard La Cote Blanche bottling for a small step up that still stays comfortably under twenty euros.

budget_wines.name: La Chablisienne Petit Chablis
budget_wines.where_to_buy: La Chablisienne cooperative, Chablis; the leading volume producer of the appellation, widely exported.
budget_wines.description: Petit Chablis from the plateau soils above the main Chablis slope, lively and steely with green-apple and citrus bite. The most affordable way into real Chablis tension under twenty euros.
budget_wines.tip: Petit Chablis is built for youth; drink the most recent vintage with oysters or shellfish rather than cellaring it.

budget_wines.name: La Chablisienne Chablis
budget_wines.where_to_buy: La Chablisienne cooperative, Chablis; broadly distributed in France, the UK and the United States.
budget_wines.description: Village Chablis from the cooperative that supplies a quarter of the appellation. Flinty, dry and mineral, the textbook unoaked expression of Kimmeridgian limestone for under twenty-five euros.
budget_wines.tip: This is the value entry to true village Chablis; the named cuvees La Pierrelee and La Sereine are the same wine under merchant labels in some markets.

budget_wines.name: Domaine A. et P. de Villaine Bouzeron
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Domaine A. et P. de Villaine, Bouzeron, Cote Chalonnaise; imported to the US by Kermit Lynch.
budget_wines.description: The reference Aligote from the only village appellation reserved for the grape, farmed organically by Aubert de Villaine of Romanee-Conti. Citrus, white-flower and a saline cut, proof Aligote is no afterthought, just under twenty-five euros.
budget_wines.tip: Bouzeron is the one place Aligote earns its own AOC; this is the bottle that converts sceptics. A classic match for snails or a gougeres aperitif.

budget_wines.name: Bouchard Aine et Fils Bourgogne Pinot Noir
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Bouchard Aine et Fils, Beaune; distributed across France and export markets.
budget_wines.description: A regional Bourgogne Rouge that delivers the bright red-cherry and light-tannin signature of Burgundian Pinot Noir at an everyday price. The easiest introduction to the grape's silky red style.
budget_wines.tip: Serve regional Bourgogne Rouge a touch cool to lift the fruit; it is built for the weeknight table, not the cellar.

budget_wines.name: Bouchard Pere et Fils Bourgogne Aligote
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Bouchard Pere et Fils, Beaune; widely available in French wine shops and abroad.
budget_wines.description: A bracing, high-acid Aligote from one of Beaune's historic houses. Lemon, green apple and a mineral snap, the original base of a kir and a sharp aperitif white for well under twenty euros.
budget_wines.tip: Aligote is the traditional base for kir with creme de cassis; on its own it shines with shellfish and fresh cheese.

budget_wines.name: La Chablisienne Cremant de Bourgogne
budget_wines.where_to_buy: La Chablisienne cooperative, Chablis; distributed through merchant and export channels.
budget_wines.description: Traditional-method Burgundian sparkling built on Chardonnay with Pinot Noir, made the same way as Champagne. Fine bubbles, brioche and citrus, a serious fizz for under twenty-five euros.
budget_wines.tip: Cremant de Bourgogne is the value alternative to Champagne for a celebration; chill well and pour as an aperitif.

budget_wines.name: Maison Louis Latour Macon-Lugny Les Genievres
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Maison Louis Latour, Beaune; one of the most distributed named Maconnais cuvees worldwide.
budget_wines.description: A single-named Macon-Lugny lieu-dit, Les Genievres, with a little more depth and breadth than the basic village wine. Ripe orchard fruit and a creamy edge, dependable value just inside twenty-five euros.
budget_wines.tip: The step from basic Macon-Lugny to Les Genievres is worth it for a dinner-party white; it carries richer dishes like roast pork or creamy pasta.
